market_context:
Cohort size: 50 listings scraped in the same market.
Median nightly rate: £104.90
Median 90-day occupancy: 48.9%
Median photo count: 23

title_variants: Sheffield City Centre 1BR – Near Tram & Theatres
analysis.rewritten_desc:
Welcome to Apartment 2 at Temple Chambers – a stylish, architect‑designed one‑bedroom apartment tucked into the historic Cathedral Quarter on pedestrian‑only Figtree Lane. This bright, airy space boasts high ceilings, large windows, and an open‑plan kitchen and living area equipped with a Smart TV and dedicated workspace. The comfortable double bedroom offers a peaceful retreat, while the modern shower room includes a WC. Guests enjoy free high‑speed Wi‑Fi, a washing machine, and a fully‑fitted kitchen for self‑catering stays.

Location is unbeatable: a two‑minute walk to Cathedral Tram Stop puts you within easy reach of the Utilita Arena, theatres, cafés, and restaurants, making it perfect for business trips, concerts, or city breaks. Paid parking is available nearby at Hartshead Square (not included).

Whether you’re staying for a night or a longer visit, you’ll find everything you need for a comfortable, convenient Sheffield experience at an exceptional rate of £64.3 per night.
analysis.photo_order_rec: Use a bright, high‑ceiling living‑area shot as the cover photo—it showcases the apartment’s most compelling features (natural light, spaciousness) and instantly captures guest interest.
key_diagnosis: Despite a nightly rate of £64.3 (39% below the city median of £104.90), occupancy is only 46.7% booked versus the cohort median of 48.9% – the low price isn’t being highlighted enough to drive bookings.

The listing has just 9 photos compared with the market median of 23; adding at least 12 high‑quality images of the bedroom, bathroom, kitchen, workspace, and building exterior will improve visual coverage.
Key guest‑expectation amenities are missing: heating/hot water, essential toiletries, and included parking – adding these will raise guest satisfaction and potential Superhost scores.
analysis.outreach_hook: Your 39% lower nightly rate (£64 vs the city median £105) is only delivering 46.7% occupancy – there’s a clear opportunity to boost bookings by highlighting this price advantage.
